# Verified-Field Operator Worksheet

## Purpose
This worksheet is a fillable operator template for the decisive fields that must be verified after listing-led screening.

It is designed to work with:
- the Planning Verification Checklist
- the Planning Source-Gathering Workflow
- the Phase 1 manual screen process

Its purpose is to make site verification faster, more consistent, and less dependent on ad hoc note-taking.

---

## Site details
- Site ID:
- Address:
- Suburb:
- Lot / Plan:
- Listing URL:
- Assessment date:
- Analyst:

## Section D — Site-strength indicators
Tick all that apply.

### Raw site strength indicators
- [ ] materially supportive site area
- [ ] corner block
- [ ] dual access / strong access signal
- [ ] explicit development/redevelopment signal
- [ ] likely supportive urban context
- [ ] prior relevant planning/approval signal

### Raw site strength classification
- [ ] Strong
- [ ] Moderate
- [ ] Weak

-

## Section E — Critical unknowns check
Mark unresolved critical fields.

- [ ] current zone
- [ ] local plan / precinct
- [ ] minimum lot size / subdivision trigger
- [ ] flood

- [ ] biodiversity / waterways

- Count:

### Secondary unknowns
- [ ] exact frontage measurement
- [ ] exact access geometry
- [ ] refined servicing detail
- [ ] indicative lot count still tentative

## Section F — Yield discipline
| Field | Value | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Assumed efficiency ratio |  | recorded / unknown |  |
| Effective yield area |  | recorded / unknown |  |
| Indicative lot count |  | stated / withheld |  |

Rule:
- do not state indicative lot count unless zone, lot size control, frontage, and major overlays are sufficiently verified

- [ ] Proceed with caution
- [ ] Hold / verify

- [ ] High

### Reason summary
1.
2.
3.

### Required next actions
1.
2.
3.

---

## Operator completion rule
This worksheet is complete when:
- decisive fields have values or explicit `unknown` status
- source references are captured
- raw site strength is classified
- critical unknown count is visible
- recommendation and confidence are stated

This worksheet is incomplete when:
- listing claims are copied without status/source
- overlays are skipped silently
- prior history is omitted on a promising site
